item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this skip a lot. it really reminds me of a few other music related skips i have read, but it still brings a refreshing originality to the table with the ignores all matter bit. It also makes me think about why it changed the audio. It could be anything from a random occurence, to a doomsday proclimation. A fat +1 from me all in all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the most ridiculous SCPs I've ever read, and I mean that in the best way. It's one of those things where the more detail goes into it, the more funny it becomes, like a Marx Brothers routine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should probably be tagged as Safe now that it's, well, Safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have some theories as to how it works
1. Infinite division(1, 0.5, 0.25, etc) it so that would explain its vanishing edge
A. it divides the size of the individual physical bits by half, and then half of the previous half: the first of the disc’s diameter has normal sized bits, the next quarter has 1/2 sized bits, the next quarter has 1/4 sized bits, and so on infinitely
B. It compacts half the amount of storage space a normal CD has into exponentially smaller spaces, possibly altering spacetime to allow more matter to fit into a smaller space
2. Extradimensional storage: it stores the excess information when not being scanned in an extradimensional space. The edge of the disc would be where the disc connects to the storage space

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the originality of it & how well it came across as written; despite the near groanworthiness of it being the Edge's recording, given how many times the phrase "the edge" was then used. Personally would've gone with a more obscure reference, perhaps a musician with a song called "the edge" or "the edge of "; maybe an early or alternate version of that song, of abnormal length, being the recording. But that's just me; this was an easy +1.

item.title: Still more revisions
item.content:encoded:
Some alterations to format and text.

I take Wu Wei at word - I agree that for objects that don't have a deep backstory or aren't a part of a planned canon or some other storyline, artificially inserting a story would be a "shoehorn" and would detract from the entry.

Like most objects in the database, this thing came into existence through as-yet unknown means. It was found by SCP personnel; it was retrieved, cataloged, made stable, studied under controlled conditions, reviewed, designated Safe, recommended as a tool, reviewed by O5, and revised.

When the "Mace Configuration" was suggested, I specifically resisted that idea. I didn't want this to be a SCP-572 / Kondraki / Incident 239-B sort of item. However, the Mace Configuration idea had merit in-universe, so I incorporated it as an O5 review as an emergency measure.

On the whole, however, SCP-2502 is what it is. What it is, especially given the anomalous second track, remains in study, available to authorized Researchers at Site-15.

item.title: Another update
item.content:encoded:
Punched up some of the wording, but most of all… I couldn't help it, I had to put Saul Hudson into the SCP. Nobody knows how that second track got there… ::X-Files theme music::

Yeah, I'm being cute and silly about it here in discussion, but I think that the mysterious addition of a new track adds a new layer to the SCP.

item.title: Re: Update!
item.content:encoded:
Additional: if anyone wonders where the idea came from, it's here - http://www.hrwiki.org/wiki/Record_Store_Day

When Strong Sad flipped the record over and it had no other side, I started thinking about non-Newtonian physics applied to solid objects in real space: how interactions of macro-scale solids that don't conform to space as we know it would impact with physical reality. The least-insane result of that thinking is this SCP. ;)

item.title: Update!
item.content:encoded:
I've edited the SCP with some of the suggestions made in this thread. I don't want to whole-hog revise it just yet, but I do like a lot of the suggestions (especially what I call the "Mace Configuration" - I really like the idea dankaar presented, and I adapted it as an alternate solution - thoughts on that?).

In general, thoughts?

As always, thanks everyone for your input! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree that this should be Safe, though I upvoted regardless because that was really the only thing that bugged me. Maybe also the fact that 100 kPa would be chosen because it is easy to maintain, not because mildly higher/lower pressure would harm the object or containment. The durability concerns are a bit weird for an object that apparently carved through the floor without harm.

Despite those quibbles, my upvote comes from the fact that this is a technically OK SCP with a novel concept (we don't have many reality-to-digital convertors, and none this literal that I recall?). I would be happy to see appropriate edits regarding the other stuff.

I would somewhat contradict the prior posts by saying that the brevity is an asset. A simple anomalous object is not likely to get dozens of upvotes (unless it's SCP-018 or something, making it a "classic"). But a novel, brief idea is a way better addition than something that goes on and on (and on) without a clear focus. So, personally, don't give this thing a backstory unless you put your heart into it. A mysterious "this just happened somehow" origin is, or should be, the norm here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This really ought to be safe class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Better containment procedure: a mounted, threaded metal rod to go thtough the central hole of the CD, and two nuts with washers to keep it suspended in the air.

The diameter of the rod should ve about as large as the hole, and the nuts and washers, when tightened, will keep it from accidentally being dislodged, while allowing enough space for it to be handled with tongs whike avoiding the non-cutting edge.

… that setup might also make a good makeshift weapon in the event of a breach or assault by a GoI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We already have a disc object with invincible cutting ability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, Roget. I've been thinking along similar lines. Since this SCP has survived the first few days, I think I'm not going to self-delete, but use addenda and judicious inline edits to fill out the story. Sort of how it would work in real documentation - new information comes in, you append the documentation. Mirror my own understanding and the growth of the story - as improved by the comments herein and my own notions - by reflecting it in the SCP.

+ Show Personal Comments
- Hide Personal Comments

To all: I'm a systems administrator, so a fair bit of my job is writing technical documentation. But knocking off the rust on the part of my brain that writes fiction is a huge part of why I applied to the Foundation: to become a better writer. Please do keep the comments coming - I'm loving how the collaborative/review aspect of this community works; it's exactly what I was looking for! :)

Anyway, that's what I'd like to do. Is this a proper way of improving a SCP, or should I self-delete and go back to sandbox?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this doesn't go far enough. It's got the basic concept down, but doesn't explore it much and needs to be expanded on. Like Deci said, I'd like to see some expansion on the connection to The Edge. I think you have the effect down, and that doesn't need to be expanded on much. What I'd like to see is more into how and why this was made, in addition to the content already here. The last addenda doesn't add much, so I'd cut that entirely or summarize it to a detail rather than an entire addenda.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a huge fan of Saul Hudson, and I'm kicking myself for not thinking of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The disc should also have a jam session between Evans and guitarist Saul Hudson.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *Very* tiny edit - un-redacted the Site location. It belongs at 15.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given the commentary from MrWrong and Decibelles, I'm considering a self-delete to bring this back to the sandbox. Voters - if you have / are going to downvote, would more story as per their suggestions change your mind, or is your downvote more of a core problem with the article / concept / what-have-you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest, it was sort of arbitrary. It was decided early on that the disc needed some sort of audio content, and I decided on The Edge because the nickname sort of goes with the effect of the disc. There's more potential there - I see what you're getting at.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This threatens to be interesting, and stops before it can be. I really wish the connection on The Edge was elaborated, because as it is from my perspective, it seems arbitrary.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah - right. There's a brief mention of that, but I see what you mean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why was it made? What intentions were there behind the object? Are there more of it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! :) Could you elaborate, please - "how it was used"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool concept that ties in infinity storage space, pi, and similar concepts. It might be a bit dull due to not addressing how it was used, but it got me interested.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First SCP attempt, many thanks to Mistbourne, sirpudding, Hihahaba and Adam Grey.
